justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model designed with cell-based architecture where no raw data crosses regions. Data residency compliance addressed for both GDPR and CCPA (treated as legal invariant enforced by construction). Global dashboard design uses anonymized metric streams without cross-region raw data access. Courier assignment regional isolation correctly analyzed as a domain invariant. Trade-offs explicitly include 3-region operational overhead (3× infrastructure cost, feature parity drift, platform team investment). Degraded EU service during outage designed with circuit breaker + stale cache pattern.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough regional isolation model — treats data residency as a structural invariant rather than policy, which is the strongest possible design stance
Global dashboard design is well-articulated: anonymized metric streams, no PII in global layer, explicit acknowledgment of eventual consistency
Courier-region affinity correctly identified as a domain invariant enforced at the aggregate level, not just middleware
Comprehensive risk analysis including feature parity drift, GeoDNS misrouting with defense-in-depth, and operational complexity
Degraded mode design includes circuit breaker, stale cache with TTLs, explicit customer-facing indicators, and clear scope (read-only, no new deliveries)
Trade-offs section explicitly considers and rejects alternatives (global DB with row-level tagging, CockroachDB geo-partitioned, active-active with CRDTs)

CCPA is not explicitly named — compliance is discussed generally as 'data residency' and 'legal invariant' rather than calling out GDPR and CCPA by name separately, though the architecture satisfies both
Identifies APAC sub-jurisdiction complexity (Japan APPI, Singapore PDPA, Australia Privacy Act) as a low-confidence area — shows mature thinking about real-world compliance
Implementation guidance is phased and pragmatic — cell-ify existing EU first, then stamp out, rather than big-bang rewrite

agent_output_excerpt:
## Executive Summary

The logistics platform requires a **federated multi-region architecture** with strict data sovereignty, region-local processing, and a global read-only aggregation layer for operations visibility. Each region (EU, US, APAC) operates as an autonomous unit owning its customer data, courier assignments, and delivery orchestration — with a lightweight global control plane that aggregates metrics without replicating PII across boundaries.
